options.text: The concentration of a hydrocarbon gas in air below which there is insufficient hydrocarbon to support combustion
The limit where there is insufficient oxygen to support combustion
The limit where there is too much oxygen to support combustion
The limit for man entry of personnel into tanks

explanation: The Lower Flammable Limit (LFL) is the lowest concentration of a gas in air that can support combustion; below this limit, the mixture is too 'lean' (insufficient fuel) to ignite.
